decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a positive-but-narrow deployment benchmark; use one-shot logprob-margin gating only if the 0.5pp loss and 20.5% cheap-acceptance tradeoff is acceptable.
decision.stop_reason: The rows400_599 benchmark now has durable accuracy and latency artifacts showing logprob/top-k gating meets the branch kill threshold while being about 6.3x faster than 3x repeated sampling.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch as a positive-but-narrow deployment benchmark; use one-shot logprob-margin gating only if the 0.5pp loss and 20.5% cheap-acceptance tradeoff is acceptable.
run_notes_tail: ### Artifacts created

- `scripts/capture_logprob_margin_benchmark.py` launches local `llama-server` instances from `/tmp/enoch_llama_services`, verifies `/models`, captures one deterministic cheap completion per row with `n_probs=10`, derives logprob/top-k margin, entropy, confidence, and token-probability signals, and stops each server after its bucket.

- `scripts/evaluate_uncertainty_gates.py` was extended to sweep logprob-specific signals: `cheap_min_token_prob`, `cheap_mean_token_prob`, and `cheap_first_token_margin` in addition to the prior confidence/margin/entropy/self-consistency fields.
- `scripts/summarize_logprob_margin_benchmark.py` writes a benchmark review comparing one-shot logprob-margin gating with the parent 3x repeated-sampling reference.
- `data/logprob_margin_benchmark/gsm8k_test_seed7_rows400_599__arithmetic_structure_v1__logprob_margin_rows.jsonl` contains 200 one-shot logprob rows.
- `data/logprob_margin_benchmark/gsm8k_test_seed7_rows400_599__question_shape_v1__logprob_margin_rows.jsonl` contains 200 one-shot logprob rows.

- `results/uncertainty_gates/gsm8k_test_seed7_rows400_599__arithmetic_structure_v1__logprob_margin_manifest.json` and `...question_shape_v1__logprob_margin_manifest.json` record server/model paths, bucket loads, `/models` verification, and latency.

- `results/uncertainty_gates/gsm8k_test_seed7_rows400_599__arithmetic_structure_v1__logprob_margin_extended_gate_summary.json` and `...question_shape_v1__logprob_margin_extended_gate_summary.json` record extended signal sweeps.
- `results/uncertainty_gates/logprob_margin_deployment_review.json` summarizes the deployment tradeoff.
### Commands run
- Smoke: `python3 scripts/capture_logprob_margin_benchmark.py ... --family arithmetic_structure_v1 --limit 2 --n-predict 16 --n-probs 10`.

- Full arithmetic capture and gate sweep: one-shot `llama-server` logprob capture over 200 rows, then `python3 scripts/evaluate_uncertainty_gates.py --max-values-per-signal 8` and the extended `--max-values-per-signal 6` sweep.
- Full question-shape capture and gate sweep: same as arithmetic over 200 rows.
- Summary: `python3 scripts/summarize_logprob_margin_benchmark.py`.

- Validation: JSON parsed for all result files and both 200-row JSONL captures; in-memory `compile(...)` syntax checks passed for scripts; `scripts/__pycache__` removed; no `llama-server` process remains running.
### Results

- Arithmetic one-shot logprob-margin gate: all-promoted accuracy 0.060, all-cheap one-shot accuracy 0.025, gated accuracy 0.055, accepted cheap fraction 0.205, recovered static-loss fraction 0.857, full capture wall time 198.90s, mean one-shot request 0.959s.

- Question-shape one-shot logprob-margin gate: all-promoted accuracy 0.060, all-cheap one-shot accuracy 0.025, gated accuracy 0.055, accepted cheap fraction 0.205, recovered static-loss fraction 0.857, full capture wall time 200.07s, mean one-shot request 0.974s.
- Parent repeated-sampling reference accepted about 0.395 / 0.390 cheap fraction with exact static-baseline recovery, but took about 1265-1267s per family for 3 stochastic samples.

- One-shot logprob-margin gating is about 6.3x faster wall-clock than the 3x repeated-sampling fallback on this setup, but accepts roughly half as many cheap rows and allows a 0.5 percentage-point accuracy loss versus all-promoted.
### Interpretation

- The branch-specific kill condition is not supported: both families meet the predefined deployment floor of within 0.5pp promoted accuracy, at least 20% cheap acceptance, and material latency improvement versus 3x sampling.

- The result is positive but narrow. It is a viable deployment-cheaper safeguard if a 0.5pp accuracy loss and 20.5% cheap acceptance are acceptable; it does not dominate repeated sampling when exact recovery and maximum cheap acceptance matter.
### Long-running tests
- Full one-shot logprob captures completed synchronously: arithmetic 198.90s, question-shape 200.07s. All helper servers were stopped after their bucket/model group.
